Nové téma: AI-assisted process risk assessment and reengineering for research-intensive organizations
Název *: Hybrid AI-Driven Decision Support Framework for Process Risk Assessment and Reengineering in Research-Intensive Organizations
Popis: The article will propose and validate a hybrid decision-support framework that uses AI-assisted document analysis, process modelling and risk scoring to identify weak points in institutional research, transfer and security-related processes and to design optimized To-Be workflows.
Výzkumná oblast: Decision support systems; business process management; operational research; institutional research governance; AI-assisted risk assessment
Možná metodologie: Design science research; BPMN/ArchiMate As-Is and To-Be process modelling; document and process analysis; hybrid fuzzy MCDM risk scoring; expert validation; university case study; sensitivity and robustness analysis
Cílový WoS obor: Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence; Operations Research & Management Science; Management; Information Science & Library Science
Očekávaný přínos: A validated framework for transforming fragmented institutional procedures into transparent, risk-aware and optimizable decision processes, with methodological contribution in AI-assisted BPM/MCDM integration and practical contribution for research governance.
Související publikace: Optimization of Production Processes using BPMN and ArchiMate; Computer aided detection of breathing disorder from ballistocardiography signal using convolutional neural network; An Agent-Based Simulation to Minimize Losses during a Terrorist Attack; Approaches combining methods of Operational Research with Business Process Model and Notation: A systematic review; Evaluation of Wind Turbine Failure Modes Using the Developed SWARA-CoCoSo Methods Based on the Spherical Fuzzy Environment; An integrated multi-criteria decision-making approach to optimize the number of leagile-sustainable suppliers in supply chains; Business process optimization for trauma planning; Sustainable resilient supplier selection for IoT implementation based on the integrated BWM and TRUST under spherical fuzzy sets; A dynamic expert system to increase patient satisfaction with an integrated approach of system dynamics, ISM, and ANP methods; Exploring decision-making techniques for evaluation and benchmarking of energy system integration frameworks for achieving a sustainable energy future; Ranking Factors Affecting Sustainable Competitive Advantage From the Business Intelligence Perspective: Using Content Analysis and F-TOPSIS; Economic production quantity model with shortages under price- and green-sensitive demand in uncertain environment; Security modules of delegation methods in mobile cloud computing using probabilistic interval neutrosophic hesitant fuzzy set based decision-making model; A hybrid decision support system for transport policy selection: A case study on Russia’s Northern Sea route in Artic region; Risk Management and Process Optimization in Industry 4.0: Integrating Sensors with Critical Path and FMEA; Cloud supply chain as a service: overcoming barriers in the fashion retail industry by developing a new cognitive map model; Comparative Analysis of AI-Integrated and Traditional Inventory Models under Uncertainty
Poznámky: Potential Q1 target journal: Expert Systems with Applications. Empirical contribution should be strengthened by a real institutional case study, expert validation and comparison with a non-AI baseline.
